ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sales_forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the scenario or model run */,
  "target_week" INTEGER /* The week for which the forecast is made */,
  "forecast_value" REAL /* The forecasted sales value */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location of the forecast */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* The end date of the forecast period */,
  "quantile" REAL /* The quantile of the forecast */,
  "truncated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the forecast value is truncated */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sales model used */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the forecast was created */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the forecast was last updated */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the forecast (e.g., 95%) */,
  "upper_bound" REAL /* Upper bound of the forecast range */,
  "lower_bound" REAL /* Lower bound of the forecast range */,
  "forecast_status" TEXT /* Status of the forecast (e.g., active, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_scenario_id FOREIGN KEY ("scenario_id") REFERENCES sales_scenarios ("scenario_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_forecasts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES sales_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_scenarios" (
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scenario */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sales scenario */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the scenario period */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the scenario period */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sales model used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scenario_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the sales location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the sales model */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the sales model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sales model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the sales model (e.g., regression, machine learning) */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the sales model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., sales manager, data analyst) */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's location */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the forecast */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the forecast was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., viewed, edited, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_access_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES sales_forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales data entry */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location of the sales data */,
  "sales_date" TEXT /* Date of the sales data */,
  "actual_sales" REAL /* Actual sales value */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated forecast */,
  "seasonal_index" REAL /* Seasonal index of the sales data */,
  "trend" REAL /* Trend of the sales data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES sales_forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model update */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sales model being updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the model was updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_updates_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES sales_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_targets" (
  "target_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales target */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the forecast being targeted */,
  "target_date" TEXT /* Date when the target was set */,
  "target_value" REAL /* Target sales value */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the target (e.g., active, achieved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("target_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_targets_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES sales_forecasts ("forecast_id")
)
